name: Page
summary: The Page class contains methods to extract information and modify the properties of an individual page. Note that inside the SketchUp user interface pages are called “Scenes”. Since SketchUp 2026.0, modifying the Axes, Camera, RenderingOptions, and ShadowInfo properties of a page is an undoable operation and should be wrapped between Model#start_operation and Model#commit_operation. Example:

methods.name: active_section_planes
methods.summary: The #active_section_planes method is used to retrieve the active section plane for the Sketchup::Page.
methods.remarks: The #active_section_planes method is used to retrieve the active section plane for the Sketchup::Page.

returns.doc: Returns nil if the page does not use section planes.

methods.name: axes
methods.summary: The axes method returns the drawing axes for the page.
methods.remarks: The axes method returns the drawing axes for the page. Since SketchUp 2026.0, modifying the axes of a scene is an undoable operation.

methods.name: camera
methods.summary: The #camera method retrieves the camera for a particular page.
methods.remarks: The #camera method retrieves the camera for a particular page. Since SketchUp 2026.0, modifying the camera properties of a scene is an undoable operation.

methods.name: get_drawingelement_visibility
methods.summary: The #get_drawingelement_visibility method is used to get the visibility of a drawing element on a particular page.
methods.remarks: The #get_drawingelement_visibility method is used to get the visibility of a drawing element on a particular page.

returns.doc: true if visible, false if not.

methods.name: hidden_entities
methods.summary: The hidden_entities method retrieves all hidden entities within a page.
methods.remarks: The hidden_entities method retrieves all hidden entities within a page.

returns.doc: an array of drawing elements that are hidden on the page.

methods.name: include_in_animation?
methods.summary: The #include_in_animation? method determines whether the page should be included when exporting an animation from the model.
methods.remarks: The #include_in_animation? method determines whether the page should be included when exporting an animation from the model.

methods.name: label
methods.summary: The label method retrieves the label for a page from the page tab.
methods.remarks: The label method retrieves the label for a page from the page tab.

returns.doc: label - a string label for the page tab

methods.name: layer_folders
methods.summary: The #layer_folders method retrieves the hidden layer folders associated with a page.
methods.remarks: The #layer_folders method retrieves the hidden layer folders associated with a page.

methods.name: layers
methods.summary: The #layers method retrieves layers that don't use their default visibility on this page.
methods.remarks: The #layers method retrieves layers that don't use their default visibility on this page.

methods.name: rendering_options
methods.summary: Note: Most rendering options of a scene are also present in Style and are governed by the selected style.
methods.remarks: Note: Most rendering options of a scene are also present in Style and are governed by the selected style. Those options should not be changed from the scene. The ones not related to Style like fog (DisplayFog, FogColor) are safe to be changed from the scene. The rendering_options method retrieves a RenderingOptions object for the page. Since SketchUp 2026.0, modifying rendering_options of a scene is an undoable operation.

methods.name: set_drawingelement_visibility
methods.summary: The #set_drawingelement_visibility method is used to change the visibility of a drawing element on a particular page.
methods.remarks: The #set_drawingelement_visibility method is used to change the visibility of a drawing element on a particular page. Only drawing elements on the root of the model, as well as nested instances of components, groups, and images are controlled by Page visibility.

methods.name: set_visibility
methods.signature: set_visibility(layer, visible_for_page) => Sketchup::Page | set_visibility(layer_folder, visible_for_page) => Sketchup::Page
methods.summary: The #set_visibility method sets the visibility for a layer or layer folder on a page.
methods.remarks: The #set_visibility method sets the visibility for a layer or layer folder on a page.

returns.doc: the page whose visibility was set.

methods.name: shadow_info
methods.summary: Note: While certain shadow settings, such as those available in the Shadows panel, can be controlled on a per-page basis, global settings like north angle, latitude, and longitude are managed at the model level and are not page-specific.
methods.remarks: Note: While certain shadow settings, such as those available in the Shadows panel, can be controlled on a per-page basis, global settings like north angle, latitude, and longitude are managed at the model level and are not page-specific. The #shadow_info method retrieves the ShadowInfo object for the page. Since SketchUp 2026.0, modifying shadow_info of a scene is an undoable operation.

methods.name: style
methods.summary: The style method retrieves the style associated with the page.
methods.remarks: The style method retrieves the style associated with the page.

returns.doc: style - the Style object if successful

methods.name: update
methods.summary: The #update method performs an update on the page properties based on the current view that the user has.
methods.remarks: The #update method performs an update on the page properties based on the current view that the user has. What properties of the Page get updated are controlled via an integer whose bits corresponds to different properties. These flags can be used individually or combined using bitwise OR.

params.name: flags
params.doc: The bitwise OR of the bit flags.

methods.name: use_axes?
methods.summary: The use_axes? method determines whether you are storing the axes property with the page.
methods.remarks: The use_axes? method determines whether you are storing the axes property with the page.

returns.doc: status - true if you are storing the this property with the page, false if you are not storing this property with the page.

methods.name: use_camera?
methods.summary: The use_camera? method determines whether you are storing the camera property with the page.
methods.remarks: The use_camera? method determines whether you are storing the camera property with the page.

returns.doc: status - true if you are storing the this property with the page, false if you are not storing this property with the page.

methods.name: use_environment?
methods.summary: The #use_environment? method is used to determine if the Environment settings are used in the scene.
methods.remarks: The #use_environment? method is used to determine if the Environment settings are used in the scene.

methods.name: use_hidden?
methods.summary: Deprecated.
methods.remarks: Deprecated. The functionality is replaced by #use_hidden_geometry? and #use_hidden_objects? in SketchUp 2020.1. The use_hidden? method determines whether you are storing the hidden property with the page.

returns.doc: status - true if you are storing the this property with the page, false if you are not storing this property with the page.

methods.name: use_hidden_geometry?
methods.summary: Returns the use hidden geometry property from the page.
methods.remarks: Returns the use hidden geometry property from the page.

methods.name: use_hidden_layers?
methods.summary: The use_hidden_layers? method determines whether you are storing the hidden layers property with the page.
methods.remarks: The use_hidden_layers? method determines whether you are storing the hidden layers property with the page.

returns.doc: status - true if you are storing the this property with the page, false if you are not storing this property with the page.

methods.name: use_hidden_objects?
methods.summary: Returns the use hidden objects property from the page.
methods.remarks: Returns the use hidden objects property from the page.

methods.name: use_rendering_options?
methods.summary: The use_rendering_options? method determines whether you are storing the rendering options property with the page.
methods.remarks: The use_rendering_options? method determines whether you are storing the rendering options property with the page.

returns.doc: status - true if you are storing the this property with the page, false if you are not storing this property with the page.

methods.name: use_section_planes?
methods.summary: The use_section_planes? method determines whether you are storing the section planes property with the page.
methods.remarks: The use_section_planes? method determines whether you are storing the section planes property with the page.

returns.doc: status - true if you are storing the this property with the page, false if you are not storing this property with the page.

methods.name: use_shadow_info?
methods.summary: The use_shadow_info? method determines whether you are storing the shadow info property with the page.
methods.remarks: The use_shadow_info? method determines whether you are storing the shadow info property with the page.

returns.doc: status - true if you are storing the this property with the page, false if you are not storing this property with the page.

methods.name: use_style?
methods.summary: The use_style? method determines whether storing a style with the page.
methods.remarks: The use_style? method determines whether storing a style with the page.

returns.doc: status - true if you are storing the this property with the page, false if you are not storing this property with the page.

properties.name: delay_time
properties.summary: The delay_time method retrieves the amount of time, in seconds, that a page will be displayed before transition to another page during a tour.
properties.remarks: The delay_time method retrieves the amount of time, in seconds, that a page will be displayed before transition to another page during a tour. The default delay time can be modified in the Model Info > Animation panel of the SketchUp User Interface. If this method returns -1, the default delay time is used.

properties.name: description
properties.summary: The description method retrieves the description for a page as found in the Scenes manager dialog.
properties.remarks: The description method retrieves the description for a page as found in the Scenes manager dialog.

properties.name: environment
properties.summary: The #environment method is used to retrieve the Environment for the scene.
properties.remarks: The #environment method is used to retrieve the Environment for the scene.

properties.name: include_in_animation
properties.summary: The #include_in_animation= method controls whether the page should be included when exporting an animation from the model.
properties.remarks: The #include_in_animation= method controls whether the page should be included when exporting an animation from the model.

properties.name: name
properties.summary: The name method retrieves the name for a page from the page tab.
properties.remarks: The name method retrieves the name for a page from the page tab.

properties.name: transition_time
properties.summary: Get the amount of time that it takes to transition to this page during a slideshow or animation export.
properties.remarks: Get the amount of time that it takes to transition to this page during a slideshow or animation export. If this value is -1, it means to use the default transition time.

properties.name: use_axes
properties.summary: The use_axes= method sets the page's axes property.
properties.remarks: The use_axes= method sets the page's axes property.

properties.name: use_camera
properties.summary: The use_camera= method sets the page's camera property.
properties.remarks: The use_camera= method sets the page's camera property.

properties.name: use_environment
properties.summary: The #use_environment= method is used to set if the Environment settings are used in the scene.
properties.remarks: The #use_environment= method is used to set if the Environment settings are used in the scene.

properties.name: use_hidden
properties.remarks: Deprecated. The functionality is replaced by #use_hidden_geometry= and #use_hidden_objects= in SketchUp 2020.1. The use_hidden= method sets the page's hidden property.

properties.name: use_hidden_geometry
properties.summary: Sets the page's use hidden geometry property.
properties.remarks: Sets the page's use hidden geometry property.

properties.name: use_hidden_layers
properties.summary: The use_hidden_layers= method sets the page's hidden layers property.
properties.remarks: The use_hidden_layers= method sets the page's hidden layers property.

properties.name: use_hidden_objects
properties.summary: Sets the page's use hidden objects property.
properties.remarks: Sets the page's use hidden objects property.

properties.name: use_rendering_options
properties.summary: The use_rendering_optoins= method sets the page's display settings property.
properties.remarks: The use_rendering_optoins= method sets the page's display settings property.

properties.name: use_section_planes
properties.summary: The use_section_planes= method sets the page's section planes property.
properties.remarks: The use_section_planes= method sets the page's section planes property.

properties.name: use_shadow_info
properties.summary: The use_shadow_info= method sets the page's shadow info property.
properties.remarks: The use_shadow_info= method sets the page's shadow info property.

properties.name: use_style
properties.summary: The use_style= method sets the style to be used by the page.
properties.remarks: The use_style= method sets the style to be used by the page.
